stdout_tail:
8v  

-- headline relaxed candidates that beat base (strict charm error) --
  none (base remains primary)

  degeneracy note:
    - `8s+` and `8s-` tie whenever only the cubic amplitude matters, because
      the current load uses `abs(sin(2π/3 * orientation))`.
    - `8v` zeroes the cubic load entirely, so any row with `top_rep=8v` leaves
      the top→charm step at the base detuned value (hence charm ≈ 1.27 GeV).
    - `charm_rep` affects the charm→up step, but not the charm sanity score.

mode unlocks (information capacity).
  • Ycum / /Y — cumulative S² harmonic degeneracy (m+1)²; /Y → 4 (octonion factor).
  • shell / K(m) — curvature imprint sample and partial curvature channel.
  • Ω_k — curvature ratio vs horizon shell (causal closure relative to outer pin).
  • sh/new — imprint per newly unlocked mode at this shell (dimensionless bookkeeping).
  • G_eff — varying gravitational coupling from φ(m) alone.
  • δθ′, N — time angle and lapse from the informational-energy axiom.

e arc are diagnostics for moiré;
    formal bounds tie to Gaussian / triangle-inequality lenses (not proved in this script).
  • Use `--json` for machine-readable output.
  • `--mod-demo` runs M mod n binary search sanity only.
  • `--phi` / `--rapidity-t` set the scalars for the Lean `polarAngleFromRapidity` mirror (default 1 / 1); `lean_rapidity_bridge` in JSON checks monotone s and cum ≤ K·Δs.
  • `--verify-omega-factorization` — optional: independent prime-factor count on M vs literal sum.

ling from `BoundStates` (GUT-normalized ladder); it is **not** α≈1 in a.u.,
    so |E0(HQIV)| is much smaller than |E0(Bohr)| unless you rescale or reinterpret.
  • For **Gaussian/minimal-basis widths** tied to **isotope mass**, use a0(std)=1/(μZ)
    and α_GTO(std); μ is the only isotope dependence at this crude level.
  • a0(HQIV) shows what happens if you literally use α_eff(m) as the Coulomb strength
    in the same length formula — useful for debugging scaling, not for STO replacement yet.
